meaning: n.放荡不羁者,玩乐者,浪子,自由思想家adj.放荡的
examples: The transition from libertine to prig was so complete.
I lost Ruth and gained an undeserved reputation as a potential libertine.
One king was a fornicating libertine, another captivated by a strange animal, while one of the queens was obsessed by her wish for a child.
Though not as libertine as Dubai, Qatar is a bastion of civil liberties--women can drive, alcohol is not illegal--compared with neighboring Saudi Arabia.
In the bat world, it seems that you do not have to be cleverer to be a libertine than to be a faithful husband.
